title: New survey finds Europeans believe vocational education and training leads to good jobs and pay
content: A new EU survey shows that Europeans view vocational education and training (VET) as a fast track to attractive and well-paid job opportunities. Most people believe VET leads to real job opportunities and solid careers.
snippet: A new EU survey shows that Europeans view vocational education and training (VET) as a fast track to attractive and well-paid job opportunities. Most people believe VET leads to real job opportunities

content: Commission accepts binding commitments by SAP to address competition concerns about services for its popular business management software*

title: Eurosat: Energy use for cooling in EU households doubled in 6 years
content: In EU households, energy consumption for cooling has steadily increased, reaching 80.4 thousand terajoules (TJ) in 2024.
snippet: In EU households, energy consumption for cooling has steadily increased, reaching 80.4 thousand terajoules (TJ) in 2024.

title: Commission adopts certification methodologies for carbon farming under the CRCF Regulation
content: The European Commission yesterday adopted three certification methodologies for carbon farming under the Carbon Removals and Carbon Farming (CRCF) Regulation.
snippet: The European Commission yesterday adopted three certification methodologies for carbon farming under the Carbon Removals and Carbon Farming (CRCF) Regulation.

title: Seveso - 50 years on: EU strengthens its commitment to industrial safety in a changing landscape of risk
content: 50 years on from the Seveso disaster in Northern Italy, the Commission has reaffirmed its commitment to preventing industrial accidents.
snippet: 50 years on from the Seveso disaster in Northern Italy, the Commission has reaffirmed its commitment to preventing industrial accidents.

title: Commissioner Kos in Montenegro to support path to EU accession
content: Today and tomorrow, Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos is in Montenegro to discuss the country's progress on its EU accession path.
snippet: Today and tomorrow, Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos is in Montenegro to discuss the country's progress on its EU accession path.

title: Commissioner Kos in North Macedonia to advance EU reforms
content: Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos is in Skopje, North Macedonia, today to discuss the country’s EU accession path and reform efforts, with a focus on implementing the Reform Agenda under the Growth Plan for the Western Balkans.

title: Commission and International Labour Organization set renewed strategic agenda for social justice, quality jobs and a human-centred future of work
content: The International Labour Organization and the European Commission reaffirmed their strategic partnership and agreed to deepen their cooperation on social justice, decent work and quality jobs at their 17th High-level meeting.

title: Turn Your Ideas into Impact for EU Agriculture with IMCAP
content: IMCAP supports communication projects that connect citizens with the Common Agricultural Policy. Through annual calls for proposals, organisations can receive EU co-funding for campaigns, events and digital initiatives across Europe.

title: Malta sees developments and research in migrant employment and diversity
content: The Council of Europe recently issued its sixth opinion on Malta, acknowledging the progress made in promoting diversity and strengthening social integration. Additionally, as of 2026, Malta has introduced a mandatory pre‑departure course for all third country nationals applying for a Single Permit.

title: Open Door Day at Koksijde Air Base showcases how Galileo supports Search and Rescue operations
content: On 8 June, around 10,000 visitors gathered at Koksijde Air Base in Belgium to celebrate 80 years of the Belgian Air Force and 65 years of the Search and Rescue Service (SAR). title: Building a circular economy with the EU Ecolabel

title: Safer cars, safer roads: New rules take effect
content: Since 7 July 2026, newly registered cars and vans are subject to more advanced safety requirements
snippet: Since 7 July 2026, newly registered cars and vans are subject to more advanced safety requirements

title: Commission seeks feedback on commitments offered by Sanofi over possible anticompetitive conduct regarding the promotion of a flu vaccine for vulnerable patients
content: The European Commission invites comments on commitments offered by Sanofi to address competition concerns regarding a communication campaign that has possibly disparaged the only rival flu vaccine recommended for vulnerable patients with risk factors.

title: New EU plan to address the risks and opportunities of advanced AI for cybersecurity
content: The European Commission has presented a plan to address the risks and harness the opportunities of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity.
snippet: The European Commission has presented a plan to address the risks and harness the opportunities of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity.

title: Setting sustainable livestock farming on a strong footing
content: The European Commission has introduced a new strategy for the livestock sector to ensure that farmers can address economic and environmental challenges sustainably in the long term.
snippet: The European Commission has introduced a new strategy for the livestock sector to ensure that farmers can address economic and environmental challenges sustainably in the long term.

title: Interconnections for South-West Europe at core of Ministerial Meeting in Paris today
content: The South-West High-Level Group on Interconnections for South-West Europe met in Paris today, bringing together energy ministers of France, Spain, Portugal and the European Commission, to advance on key Trans-Pyrenean interconnection projects, offshore energy and hydrogen.

title: Portugal achieves major milestone with permit for offshore aquaculture facility
content: Mariculture Systems, a company based in Ramat Negev, Israel, has announced that it has obtained an Aquaculture Activity Title (Ttulo de Atividade Aqucola - TAA) from the Portuguese authorities.
snippet: Mariculture Systems, a company based in Ramat Negev, Israel, has announced that it has obtained an Aquaculture Activity Title (Ttulo de Atividade Aqucola - TAA) from the Portuguese authorities.

title: Quarterly reports show a surge in solar energy and e-vehicles, with record highs in 2025
content: The latest quarterly reports for the electricity and gas markets, covering the final two quarters of 2025, confirm continued growth in the generation of renewable energy in the EU.
snippet: The latest quarterly reports for the electricity and gas markets, covering the final two quarters of 2025, confirm continued growth in the generation of renewable energy in the EU.

title: Europe’s job market is shifting, and EURES is ready to lead the way into 2030
content: The new EURES strategy sets a clear direction to make finding work and workforce needs easier, fairer and more connected across Europe.
snippet: The new EURES strategy sets a clear direction to make finding work and workforce needs easier, fairer and more connected across Europe.

title: From a remote island to shelves around the world: the story of Azorean canned fish
content: The island of Terceira in the Azores sits 1,500 km west of mainland Portugal – remote enough to make running a business complicated, let alone one built around delivering fresh fish to distant markets. Tibério Barbeito started TZT Flying Fish Azores in his twenties with exactly that ambition.

title: Commission proposes five joint defence projects to strengthen Europe's industrial capabilities
content: Today, on 3rd July 2026, the European Commission proposed five new large-scale projects, known as the European Defence Projects of Common Interest (EDPCIs), to help EU countries strengthen their defence capabilities by developing key military systems together.

title: European Commission’s Annual Tax Report (ART) provides latest insights into the revenue mix
content: The European Commission has presented its Annual Report on Taxation (ART), offering a detailed overview of taxation policies in all EU countries. This report assesses recent trends in EU tax systems and identifies ways to enhance compliance.
snippet: The European Commission has presented its Annual Report on Taxation (ART), offering a detailed overview of taxation policies in all EU countries. This report assesses recent trends in EU tax systems

title: Commission issues guidance to strengthen resilience of critical infrastructure
content: On 10 July, the Commission adopted a Communication providing EU countries and their critical entities with guidance on technical, security and organisational measures to strengthen the resilience of critical infrastructure, supporting the implementation of the Critical Entities Resilience Directive.

title: EU clarifies rules to allow continued waste recovery shipments to Switzerland
content: The amendment ensures that long-standing and sustainable waste management arrangements between the EU & Switzerland can continue.
snippet: The amendment ensures that long-standing and sustainable waste management arrangements between the EU & Switzerland can continue.

title: Poland sees new research and changes to policies on migrant integration
content: Employment visa applicants in shortage occupations will be prioritised in Poland, while around 100 prosecutors are being trained to tackle bias crimes against migrants. Temporary protection for people displaced from Ukraine is extended to 4 March 2027.

title: Joint statement by High Representative/Vice-President Kallas and Commissioner Kos on the 31st anniversary of the Srebrenica genocide
content: Tomorrow, Europe pays its tribute to the memory of over 8,300 Bosniak men and boys who were killed in the Srebrenica genocide 31 years ago. Our thoughts are with the families of the victims of the genocide.

title: Shared commitment to advance EOSC through new EOSC Nodes and EOSC projects
content: 28 EOSC Nodes in the EOSC Federation and 34 EOSC projects under Horizon Europe met to review EOSC progress and strengthen collaboration for its next phase.
snippet: 28 EOSC Nodes in the EOSC Federation and 34 EOSC projects under Horizon Europe met to review EOSC progress and strengthen collaboration for its next phase.

title: Europe's innovation performance strengthens amidst global competition
content: Europe's innovation performance continues to improve, with the EU's overall score rising by 11.6 percentage points since 2019, according to the 2026 European Innovation Scoreboard (EIS), just released by the European Commission.

title: Why young jobseekers struggle to find roles abroad that fit their expertise
content: A new EU report sheds light on how young people moving within Europe find work, and examines how well their jobs match their qualifications and skills.
snippet: A new EU report sheds light on how young people moving within Europe find work, and examines how well their jobs match their qualifications and skills.

title: Commission imposes anti-dumping duties on passenger car and light lorry tyres from China
content: On 7 July 2026, the Commission published a regulation imposing definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of passenger car and light lorry tyres originating in the People’s Republic of China.
snippet: On 7 July 2026, the Commission published a regulation imposing definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of passenger car and light lorry tyres originating in the People’s Republic of China.

title: 5 things you should know about extreme heat – and how to beat it
content: Extreme heat is a growing challenge across Europe. In the first half of this year, Europe has already experienced two major heatwaves, with temperatures in June of up to 41°C for 100 million Europeans and all-time heat records beaten in thousands of cities.
snippet: Extreme heat is a growing challenge across Europe. In the first half of this year, Europe has already experienced two major heatwaves, with temperatures in June of up to 41°C for 100 million Europeans

title: 9th Seminar of European Customs Chemists
content: The 9th Seminar of European Customs Chemists (9SECC) took place in Stockholm, Sweden, from 1 to 3 July 2026 under the theme ‘Fostering Unity in Uncertain Times’.
snippet: The 9th Seminar of European Customs Chemists (9SECC) took place in Stockholm, Sweden, from 1 to 3 July 2026 under the theme ‘Fostering Unity in Uncertain Times’.

title: Latest developments on the protection of third country nationals and labour integration in Italy
content: Italy has announced the extension of temporary protection for people displaced from Ukraine, while a new agreement was concluded between the IOM and the National Labour Inspector to protect victims of labour exploitation. The OECD published a 2026 assessment of migrant integration policies in Italy.

title: EU and South Africa kick off intergovernmental dialogue on Clean Trade and Investment Partnership
content: Today, the EU and the South Africa are holding the first government-to-government dialogue at senior-official level to advance on the implementation of their Clean Trade and Investment Partnership (CTIP).

title: Commission adopts Implementing Regulation to strengthen sustainable urban mobility through harmonised indicators
content: The European Commission has today adopted an Implementing Regulation establishing a common set of urban mobility indicators to be applied across the European Union.
snippet: The European Commission has today adopted an Implementing Regulation establishing a common set of urban mobility indicators to be applied across the European Union.

title: Farmers, fair markets and consumers: new report highlights the role of EU competition rules in agriculture
content: The Commission published its periodic report on the application of EU competition rules in the agricultural sector. The report shows that these rules give farmers opportunities to work together, strengthen their position and contribute to fairer agri-food markets.
